method: Normal CDF N(mu=1.835, sigma=0.025). HARD ANCHOR: yesterday's KXTRUFCPI-26APR27 finalized YES on T1.80 and NO on T1.85, so Apr 27 Truflation print was in (1.80, 1.85] -- consistent with reported 1.83. Apr 28 anchor = 1.83 + tiny gas uplift (AAA +1.6c d/d, $4.111->$4.176 = +0.4% d/d, contributes ~+0.005pp via retail basket). Sigma 0.025 absorbs: (a) we don't know yesterday's exact value within the 5c bucket, (b) intraday Truflation methodology drift, (c) gas pass-through uncertainty. Major correction from prior mu=1.81 sigma=0.06 which over-relied on stale Apr-17 Truflation blog datapoint and ignored the day-N-1 Kalshi resolution evidence.
notes: HARD RECALIBRATION 2026-04-28: prior theo (mu=1.81, sigma=0.06) was wildly mispriced -- T1.76 NO showed 0.20 when day-N-1 Kalshi resolution proved the value sits ~1.83 (5+ sigma above 1.76 under any reasonable daily-move assumption). New strikes via N(1.835, 0.025).
